metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `00XX` placeholder in the AC and Files section with a concrete provisional number (e.g., the next available like 0039) or reword the AC to check for any `*transport-retry.md` file under `docs/adr/`, so CI can verify it without knowing the final number.
findings.finding: The ADR path `docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md` uses a literal placeholder (`00XX`) that cannot be resolved by the implementing agent or CI. The AC says 'an ADR stub exists at the placeholder path' — but a path with a literal `00XX` cannot be verified by a filesystem check or `make test`.
findings.location: AC: 'an ADR stub exists at the placeholder path WITH real content'; plan ADR section: 'docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md (the 00XX number is a placeholder…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC to: 'an ADR stub exists at `docs/adr/00[3-9][0-9]-transport-retry.md` (or a specific provisional number agreed at plan-write time) with real content…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the ADR path ambiguity: either (a) state a concrete interim filename (e.g. `docs/adr/DRAFT-transport-retry.md`) that the implementer commits and the AC can assert on, with blackbear renaming at integration; or (b) remove the path-existence assertion from CI and replace it with a PR checklist item, so the AC is verifiable without knowing the final number.
findings.finding: The ADR path `docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md` uses a literal placeholder where `00XX` is not assigned until blackbear integrates; an implementing agent cannot write a verifiable proving command (e.g. `ls docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md`) against an unknown filename, and CI cannot assert the stub's existence at a path that isn't fixed at implementation time.
findings.location: AC: 'an ADR stub exists at the placeholder path WITH real content'; Section ADR; Files list: `docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md`
findings.suggested_fix: Use `docs/adr/DRAFT-transport-retry.md` as the committed filename; add a note that blackbear renames to the final `00XX` at integration. Update the AC to assert `ls docs/adr/DRAFT-transport-retry.md` (or equivalent `test -f`) so CI can verify the stub exists in the closing PR.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly injects an `httpx.NetworkError` (e.g., `httpx.ConnectError`) and an `httpx.TimeoutException` into the mock transport and asserts that each triggers at least one retry (attempt count ≥ 2) before the 200 recovery, validating the `retry_if_exception_type` predicates for these non-status-code paths.
findings.finding: The plan claims httpx.NetworkError (ConnectError/ReadError/WriteError) and httpx.TimeoutException are retried via the RetryConfig predicate, but no AC specifies a test that actually injects one of these exception types and asserts a retry occurs. The spike and integration ACs cover only HTTPStatusError paths (429→200, 500→200). If the retry_if_exception_type predicates for NetworkError/TimeoutException are mis-wired or omitted, the entire test suite passes while these failure paths silently fall through unretried.
findings.location: AC line 4 ('529 + the transient set … + httpx timeout/network (NetworkError: connect/read/write) are retried') and Retry config section
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the spike/integration test with two additional sub-cases: (1) a transport stub that raises `httpx.ConnectError` on attempt 1 then returns 200 on attempt 2 — assert `requests == 2`; (2) a transport stub that raises `httpx.ReadTimeout` on attempt 1 then returns 200 on attempt 2 — assert `requests == 2`. Add corresponding AC bullets mirroring the existing 429 and 500 spike bullets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the body ('exhaustion is logged where `reraise` surfaces') and AC7 ('this story does NOT add a second exhaustion log with the retry prefix'): either clarify that 'where `reraise` surfaces' means the pre-existing except seam catches it (no new log needed), or explicitly state that the before_sleep callback covers only per-attempt logging and exhaustion is handled solely by runner.py:353-365.
findings.finding: The body's 'Retry observability' section states 'exhaustion is logged where `reraise` surfaces,' which an implementer could reasonably read as a directive to add a new exhaustion log at the tenacity re-raise point. AC7 then explicitly contradicts this: 'this story does NOT add a second exhaustion log with the retry prefix; the pre-existing generic except seam (runner.py:353-365) which already logs the failure' handles exhaustion. An implementer following the body may add a new explicit exhaustion log; one following the AC will not — producing divergent implementations.
findings.location: Plan body 'Retry observability' section vs AC item 7 (last bullet block)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Retry observability' section, replace 'exhaustion is logged where `reraise` surfaces' with 'exhaustion is surfaced at the pre-existing except seam (runner.py:353-365) which catches the re-raised exception — no new exhaustion log is added by this story.' This aligns body and AC7 unambiguously.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether this story's `except Exception` seam at `runner.py:353-365` should also defensively catch/unwrap `tenacity.RetryError` (wrapping the cause as the original exception), or whether `reraise=True` is guaranteed sufficient and RetryError unwrapping is exclusively mamba's domain.
findings.finding: The exhaustion-path handling has an unresolved co-location ambiguity: the plan simultaneously states (a) 'reraise=True re-raises the last attempt's ORIGINAL exception on exhaustion' and (b) 'story mamba's classifier additionally unwraps a tenacity.RetryError defensively for the edge paths.' If reraise=True always surfaces the original httpx exception, RetryError never reaches runner.py:353-365's except Exception seam — yet the plan leaves open whether this story's except Exception seam should also defensively unwrap RetryError, or whether that is entirely mamba's responsibility. The decision rule governing this boundary is not co-located with the exhaustion-handling protocol.
findings.location: Plan section 'Retry config (proven by the spike)' (reraise=True note) and AC line 'EXHAUSTION (all attempts fail) is surfaced by the pre-existing generic except seam (runner.py:353-365)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a WARNING-level exhaustion log (e.g., via tenacity's `after` callback or a try/except around the transport call) that emits 'llm transport retry exhausted after N attempts: <exception>' with the retry attempt count, distinct from the per-attempt before_sleep logs, so operators can identify exhausted retries in a single log line.
findings.finding: Exhaustion observability gap: when all retry attempts are exhausted, the plan delegates to the pre-existing generic except seam (runner.py:353-365) which logs the raw exception with no retry context (attempt count, which statuses triggered retries). The before_sleep callback logs individual attempts, but the terminal exhaustion event carries no 'retried N times before giving up' context, making triage harder for operators.
findings.location: AC final bullet: 'EXHAUSTION (all attempts fail) is surfaced by the pre-existing generic except seam (runner.py:353-365) which already logs the failure — this story does NOT add a second exhaustion log with the retry prefix'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `after=after_log(logger, logging.WARNING)` or a custom `after` callback to the RetryConfig that logs 'llm transport retry exhausted after {retry_state.attempt_number} attempts' before reraise surfaces the exception; assert this log line appears in the exhaustion test case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `http_timeout` parameter on `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` should be deferred to the hoopoe story (which owns that timeout object) rather than pre-wired here as a YAGNI parameter — or explicitly justify why the forward-compatible interface is worth the coupling.
findings.finding: The `http_timeout: httpx.Timeout | None = None` parameter on `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` is designed exclusively for the future hoopoe story, which is not in scope for this delivery. At the time this story lands, the helper has exactly one call site and the parameter has no non-default caller — a YAGNI interface surface added preemptively for a dependency that doesn't exist yet.
findings.location: Plan section: 'Timeout semantics + the hoopoe merge interface'; `_build_retrying_anthropic_model(name, *, base_url, cfg, http_timeout: httpx.Timeout | None = None)`
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the `http_timeout` parameter from this story's helper; have hoopoe's story add it when it lands (or have hoopoe's story own the helper refactor). The non-None default `httpx.Timeout(cfg.timeout_s)` is sufficient for this story's ACs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/interfaces/store/test_execution_mode_dispatch.py` to the plan's edit list and specify that it needs a patch for the new `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` call site (e.g., stub it out alongside the existing stubs).
findings.finding: The plan's unified-helper refactor (constructing the retrying AnthropicModel on the normal path, not just the loopback path) will make `tests/interfaces/store/test_execution_mode_dispatch.py` break: that test patches `_local_proxy_bypass_base_url` to return `None` and `_pai_model` to return `"anthropic:fake"`, but does NOT patch the new `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` helper. With the refactor, the normal anthropic path will now call the helper even when `_local_proxy_bypass_base_url()` is None, importing real `AsyncAnthropic`/`AnthropicProvider` in a stub-heavy test environment.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `monkeypatch.setattr(runner_mod, '_build_retrying_anthropic_model', lambda name, *, base_url, cfg, http_timeout=None: f'anthropic:{name}')` alongside the existing stubs in that test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a numbered experiment-log entry (e.g. [7]) with a captured output snippet confirming `asyncio.run(http_client.aclose())` after `agent.run_sync()` produces no `ResourceWarning` or unclosed-socket warning in the ThreadPoolExecutor / anyio-loop context, matching the evidence standard of log entries [1]-[6].
findings.finding: The `asyncio.run(http_client.aclose())` post-`run_sync` teardown is asserted as 'VERIFIED offline' but lacks a captured output snippet in the experiment log (unlike the other 6 numbered log entries), making it the only lifecycle claim without an evidence-class-equivalent proof. The concern is real: `run_sync` internally creates an anyio event loop; after it tears down, `asyncio.run(aclose())` starts a fresh stdlib asyncio loop to drain an httpx connection pool that was opened by anyio's asyncio backend — this works in practice but the plan's 'verified' claim is weaker than its other spike results.
findings.location: AsyncClient lifecycle section / 'Notes on prior art' bullet; AC: 'no unclosed-client resource warning across a run'
findings.suggested_fix: Run the spike teardown scenario under a ThreadPoolExecutor (not just on the main thread), capture `warnings.catch_warnings(record=True)` output, and add it as log entry [7]. If a warning does fire, replace `asyncio.run(aclose())` with the sync `httpx.Client` adapter or a `try: loop.run_until_complete(aclose()) finally: loop.close()` pattern using the loop that ran `run_sync`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rationale to the plan (or a named live-smoke AC) addressing why no end-to-end live-Anthropic AC is included — e.g. 'a live smoke test is impractical because 429s cannot be forced from the real API; instead, the spike's architectural proof plus the construction-time guard assert the transport is installed, and operational monitoring detects regression' — so E6(f) is satisfied by documented rationale rather than an absent criterion.
findings.finding: The plan cuts over retry handling for ALL real Anthropic API calls (both normal and loopback paths) from SDK-default retries to the new transport layer, but no acceptance criterion exercises this cutover against the live Anthropic API endpoint — every AC verifies offline/mocked transport behavior only (the spike uses a local mock server, the guard test forces injection failure, the caplog test uses a mock). E6(f) requires that a cutover to a new code path have at least one AC that exercises it end-to-end as in production, or an explicit rationale for its absence.
findings.location: AC: spike integration test (429->200); Section 'The single unified Anthropic construction'; Scope; E6 criterion (f)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan note under Scope or the spike AC: 'End-to-end live exercise: a post-merge canary run (or the next real agent gate run) serves as the live validation; forcing a 429 from the production Anthropic API is not reproducible in CI. The construction-time guard (max_retries==0 assertion + transport-present assertion) provides the strongest in-session proxy.' This makes the absence of a live AC an explicit, reasoned decision rather than an oversight.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` helper's signature and cross-story contract (what hoopoe must pass, what defaults apply, what invariants it upholds) to the required ADR content so the inter-story seam is durably documented, not just in plan prose.
findings.finding: The `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` helper is described as a cross-story interface contract (hoopoe explicitly passes `http_timeout` into it), but the ADR content spec in the AC lists only 'Context / Decision / Consequences: the transport-retry-with-SDK-max_retries=0 decision and its side-effect-safety invariant' — the helper's signature and its contract as an inter-story seam are not listed as required ADR content, leaving no durable, discoverable artifact for hoopoe's author or future maintainers beyond plan prose.
findings.location: ADR content AC: 'an ADR stub exists at the placeholder path WITH real content (Context / Decision / Consequences: the transport-retry-with-SDK-max_retries=0 decision and its side-effect-safety invariant)'; Scope section: '_build_retrying_anthropic_model(name, *, base_url, cfg, http_timeout: httpx.Timeout | None = None)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the ADR content AC to: '…side-effect-safety invariant, AND the `_build_retrying_anthropic_model` helper contract (signature, caller responsibilities, defaults)' — so hoopoe's author has a single canonical reference at `docs/adr/00XX-transport-retry.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollout procedure note (e.g., 'deploy with REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1 to validate transport injection health before enabling retries') so operators have a defined staged-enable path, not just a rollback path.
findings.finding: Staged rollout procedure is implicit but not explicitly documented: the plan describes a rollback env-var (REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1) but does not specify the rollout procedure (e.g., 'deploy with flag=1 to observe transport injection is healthy, then flip to 4'), meaning the behavior change could reach 100% of production traffic in a single deploy with retries enabled.
findings.location: Config keys section: 'Rollback / back-out: setting cfg.llm_retry_max_attempts=1 ... is the operational back-out'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Rollout procedure' bullet under Config keys: 'Initial deploy: set REBAR_LLM_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1 (fail-fast, transport installed but no retry) to validate injection; once confirmed healthy, set to 4 to enable retries.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `tmp/spike.py` is a committed test (give its final path under `tests/`) or an ephemeral local proof; if the former, add it to the named file list in the plan's Files section.
findings.finding: The spike artifact `tmp/spike.py` is referenced as a citable proof file, but no such path exists in the repository. If the AC's 'spike integration test proves a 429→200 sequence is retried' maps to a committed test file, the file is absent; if it is an ephemeral local script, the AC cannot be verified by CI.
findings.location: AC: 'The spike integration test proves a 429(+Retry-After)->200 sequence…'; plan body: 'SPIKE (the load-bearing proof; tmp/spike.py, re-run offline)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'tmp/spike.py, re-run offline' with the committed path, e.g., `tests/integration/test_transport_retry_spike.py`, and add that path to the 'Files' section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harden the `httpx.AsyncClient` teardown spec: document that `asyncio.run(aclose())` requires no running loop on the calling thread (true today because anyio tears it down before `run_sync` returns), and add a guard or alternative teardown path (e.g., `loop.run_until_complete` when a loop is already present, or synchronous `http_client._transport.close()`) so the AC test explicitly covers the teardown from a thread-pool context.
findings.finding: The `asyncio.run(http_client.aclose())` teardown mechanism is specified without acknowledging its hidden precondition: it only works safely when no event loop is running on the calling thread at teardown time. This holds today (anyio tears down its loop before `run_sync` returns), but the plan's AC and spec do not make this invariant explicit, leaving a latent correctness trap if a future integration calls `runner.run()` from an async-bridged context (e.g., `asyncio.to_thread(runner.run, req)` from an async caller). The AC currently asserts only 'no unclosed-client warning', which would not catch `RuntimeError: This event loop is already running` in a bridged-async caller scenario.
findings.location: AsyncClient lifecycle section; AC line: 'no unclosed-client resource warning across a run'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Constrain the `before_sleep` callback to log ONLY safe fields (HTTP status code, attempt number, sleep seconds, and — if present — the `Retry-After` header value); explicitly prohibit logging the exception repr, request headers, or request body; assert in the caplog test that the `x-api-key` string does NOT appear in any log record produced during a retry sequence.
findings.finding: The `before_sleep` observability callback introduced by this plan logs retry attempt details (status, attempt number, sleep duration), but the plan does not declare that the callback EXCLUDES request headers or bodies. An `httpx.HTTPStatusError` carries the originating `request` object, whose `.headers` mapping contains the `x-api-key` / `Authorization` header (the Anthropic API key). An incautious `str(exc)` or `repr(exc)` in the callback body would write the API key to the application log on every retry attempt — a secret-in-logs exposure on the outbound-to-Anthropic trust boundary.
findings.location: ### Retry observability / AC: 'Each retry ATTEMPT is logged at WARNING … asserted via caplog matching that substring'
findings.suggested_fix:
In the callback implementation, extract only safe fields explicitly:
```python
def _before_sleep(retry_state: RetryCallState) -> None:
    exc = retry_state.outcome.exception()
    status = exc.response.status_code if isinstance(exc, httpx.HTTPStatusError) else type(exc).__name__
    sleep_s = getattr(retry_state.next_action, 'sleep', '?')
    logger.warning(
        'llm transport retry: attempt %d status %s sleeping %ss',
        retry_state.attempt_number, status, sleep_s
    )
```
And add to the caplog AC test: `assert 'x-api-key' not in caplog.text` and `assert 'authorization' not in caplog.text.lower()`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan note that explicitly defines the retry/guard protocol for non-anthropic model paths (e.g. 'non-anthropic paths are out of scope for this story and retain SDK default retry behavior; the guard fires only when resolved.startswith("anthropic")') so the implementer has an authoritative decision rule.
findings.finding: The construction-time guard and unified retrying-client helper are specified only for anthropic models; the plan never defines the fallback protocol for non-anthropic models (OpenAI, Gemini) that are valid runtime paths per the codebase's _PAI_PROVIDER_PREFIX table. An implementer cannot determine whether: (a) the guard should simply skip non-anthropic paths silently, (b) non-anthropic SDK default retries remain in effect (a deliberately tolerated divergence), or (c) a separate retrying transport should be wired for those providers too.
findings.location: Plan section 'The single unified Anthropic construction'; AC line 'The unified helper builds the retrying client for ANY anthropic model'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'The single unified Anthropic construction' section: 'Non-anthropic model paths (OpenAI, Gemini) are out of scope for this story and retain their SDK-default retry behavior unchanged; the construction-time guard is gated on `resolved.startswith("anthropic")` and does not fire for other providers.'

findings.finding: The AC7 observability assertion requires caplog to match the `llm transport retry` prefix for BOTH retry attempts AND exhaustion, but the 'Retry observability' section only pins that prefix to the `before_sleep` callback (retry attempts). The exhaustion log site — where `reraise` surfaces and the plan says 'exhaustion is logged where reraise surfaces' — is never given a concrete log message format or the `llm transport retry` prefix. An implementer writing the exhaustion logger and the test asserting it have no single authoritative spec for the exhaustion log line's prefix/format.
findings.location: Plan section 'Retry observability'; AC line 7: 'the AsyncClient does not leak ... an ADR stub exists at the placeholder path; Retry attempts + exhaustion are logged at WARNING on the rebar.llm.runner logger with a stable llm transport retry prefix ... asserted via caplog matching that substring'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Retry observability' section, add: 'Exhaustion is logged at WARNING with the same prefix: `llm transport retry: exhausted after <n> attempts: <exc>`'. Update AC7 to quote this template explicitly.

findings.finding: AC8 asserts caplog will match the `llm transport retry` prefix for BOTH retry attempts AND exhaustion, but the What/observability section only specifies that prefix for the `before_sleep` callback; the exhaustion log is described only as occurring 'where `reraise` surfaces' with no specified prefix — leaving the AC assertion underspecified and potentially unverifiable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Retry observability subsection: 'The exhaustion catch-site in `PydanticAIRunner.run()` also logs at WARNING with the same `llm transport retry` prefix: `llm transport retry: exhausted after <n> attempts — <exc>`; this is the log line AC8's caplog assertion matches on the exhaustion path.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
